sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Global economic review 2022
feed.summary: This article provides a global economic review for the year 2022. It presents an overview of key economic trends and developments that shaped the global financial landscape throughout that period.

feed.title: Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) Price Target Raised to $112.00 at TD Cowen
feed.summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Church & Dwight (NYSE:CHD) to $112.00 from $99.00, maintaining a "hold" rating and indicating a potential upside of 8.60%. This follows Church & Dwight's Q4 earnings beat, with EPS at $0.86 against an estimated $0.84, and a 3.9% year-over-year revenue increase. The company also provided FY2026 EPS guidance of $3.71–$3.81.

feed.title: UBS Group Lowers KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) Price Target to $125.00
feed.summary: UBS Group has reduced its price target for KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) to $125.00 from $168.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating. This new target implies a potential upside of 24.09% from the stock's previous close. Despite missing EPS estimates slightly in the latest quarter, KKR saw a significant 76.1% year-over-year revenue increase, and insiders have been actively purchasing shares.

feed.title: Top coal and consumable fuels stocks by year-to-date performance
feed.summary: This article identifies the top-performing stocks in the coal and consumable fuels sector based on their year-to-date performance. The content is sourced from MSN, suggesting a focus on market updates and financial analysis within this industry.

feed.title: VESTA: Double-digit rental revenue growth, record renewals, and improved margins drive strong 2025 results
feed.summary: VESTA reported strong 2025 results, characterized by double-digit rental revenue growth, record leasing renewals, and enhanced margins. The company also made significant debt repayments, improving its financial flexibility. VESTA's 2026 guidance anticipates continued robust revenue and margin performance.

feed.title: Mueller Industries (MLI) Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 40%
feed.summary: Mueller Industries (MLI) has increased its quarterly dividend by 40% to 35 cents per share, reflecting strong financial health with robust margins, a solid balance sheet, and minimal leverage. The company, a prominent player in the Industrials sector specializing in copper, brass, aluminum, and plastic products, demonstrates consistent financial performance. Despite some insider selling, the overall financial position remains strong, supported by high profitability and liquidity metrics, with a fair valuation and positive analyst outlook.

feed.title: Federal agency sues Coca-Cola distributor for excluding men from Mohegan Sun work event
feed.summary: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) has filed a lawsuit against Coca-Cola Beverages Northeast Inc. for allegedly violating federal discrimination laws. The lawsuit claims the company excluded male employees from a networking event held at Mohegan Sun Casino by inviting only female employees. Coca-Cola Beverages Northeast, however, stated that the event complied with existing EEOC regulations and expressed disappointment that the EEOC did not conduct a full investigation.

feed.title: Intuit Inc. (INTU) Launched a New Campaign with Uber Advertising
feed.summary: Intuit Inc. (INTU) has partnered with Uber Advertising to launch a new campaign aimed at making in-person tax help more accessible. This initiative, part of Intuit's broader strategy to expand its Human Intelligence service network, offers free Uber rides to customers meeting with TurboTax experts. Intuit also recently launched an AI-driven construction addition to its Enterprise Suite, targeting the $2 trillion construction market.

feed.title: S&P Global (SPGI) Enters a Strategic Data Sharing Agreement with Verisk
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) has announced a strategic data-sharing agreement with Verisk to provide climate catastrophe risk analytics for the insurance and finance sectors. This partnership combines S&P's climate projections with Verisk's insurance modeling to help quantify insured and uninsured losses from future climate events. The agreement includes integrating Verisk's risk data into S&P Global's platform, utilizing Verisk's Touchstone for flood projections, and offering "decision-grade" metrics for portfolio stress testing.

feed.title: Federal agency sues Coca-Cola distributor for excluding men from Mohegan Sun work event
feed.summary: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) has filed a lawsuit against Coca-Cola Beverages Northeast Inc. for allegedly violating federal discrimination laws. The lawsuit claims the company excluded male employees from a two-day networking event held at the Mohegan Sun casino in September 2024, which was exclusively for female employees. While Coca-Cola Beverages Northeast maintains the event complied with EEOC regulations and expressed disappointment in the lawsuit, the EEOC asserts that excluding men from an employer-sponsored event is a violation of Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.

feed.title: What’s Next For Fortinet Stock?
feed.summary: Fortinet (FTNT) reported strong financial results for fiscal year 2025, with revenue up 14% to $6.80 billion and billings climbing 16% to $7.55 billion. The cybersecurity firm demonstrated robust profitability with a 37% non-GAAP operating margin and $2.21 billion in free cash flow, driven by strong demand for its solutions and a growing pipeline of recurring revenue from services. Looking ahead to 2026, Fortinet anticipates continued expansion, fueled by increasing enterprise deals, growth in unified SASE and AI-linked security solutions, and its strong cash position.

feed.title: Apple Just Took a Page Straight Out of the Netflix Playbook. Here's Why It Could Be a Brilliant Move
feed.summary: Apple has adopted a strategy similar to Netflix's by acquiring full ownership of its hit series "Severance." This move allows Apple to produce future seasons in-house and potentially expand the franchise, aiming to avoid production delays and build its premium content library. The company's long-term strategy focuses on high-quality, creator-driven content to establish Apple TV+ as a premium brand, mirroring its successful approach with other Apple products.

feed.title: KKR’s Top Executives Shake Off Industry Fears, Buy Up $35 Million in Stock
feed.summary: Executives at KKR, a private-equity giant, have purchased $35 million in company stock, signaling strong confidence despite a challenging start to the year for alternative asset managers. This move aims to reassure investors about the health of their portfolios. The significant investment by top executives demonstrates their belief in the firm's future.

feed.title: CAGNY 2026 day four: Science, scale, and the art of selective growth
feed.summary: Day four of CAGNY 2026 highlighted how CPG companies approach growth, with presentations from Colgate-Palmolive, L'Oréal, Vita Coco, and Newell Brands. Key themes included the varying maturity of AI adoption, the importance of science-backed differentiation, and the challenge of balancing pricing discipline with volume recovery through innovation. The report emphasizes that CPG partners are diverse, requiring tailored commercial partnerships.

feed.title: Important takeaways from Medtronic’s (MDT) Q3 2026 earnings report
feed.summary: Shares of Medtronic Plc (NYSE: MDT) experienced a decline despite the company reporting better-than-expected Q3 2026 earnings. Investors were concerned about weaker margins and a cautious full-year outlook. However, Medtronic continues its tradition as a Dividend Aristocrat, signaling strong cash-flow generation and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Graphic Packaging CEO to brief investors at Raymond James conference March 4
feed.summary: Graphic Packaging Holding Company announced that its President and CEO, Robbert Rietbroek, and interim CFO, Charles Lischer, will present at the Raymond James Institutional Investors Conference on March 4, 2026, at 9:50 AM ET. The presentation time has been updated from prior listings and will be available live and as an archived webcast on the company's investor relations website. This update follows a period of softer financial results for Graphic Packaging in 2025, with net sales and income declining from 2024 levels, and the stock experiencing a significant drop after its last earnings release.

feed.title: Easy Income Portfolio: February 2026 Edition
feed.summary: The February 2026 Easy Income Portfolio focuses on robust income streams despite market sentiment fluctuations. It highlights the resilience of private credit, energy income, and mortgage-backed securities, arguing that current market fear around areas like AI's impact on software lending is an overreaction rather than fundamental deterioration. The portfolio emphasizes diversified, structured investments like preferred stocks, closed-end funds, and various debt instruments, aiming for consistent cash flow and potential upside from mispricing.

feed.title: Teva Pharmaceuticals Seeks FDA Greenlight For New Schizophrenia Treatment
feed.summary: Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. (TEVA)'s U.S. affiliate announced that the FDA has accepted its New Drug Application (NDA) for TEV-'749, an extended-release injectable suspension of olanzapine, for the treatment of schizophrenia in adults. This application is supported by late-stage trial data demonstrating the once-monthly subcutaneous injection's efficacy and safety profile aligns with existing olanzapine formulations, without requiring post-injection monitoring. Following the announcement, shares of TEVA experienced a 1% decline.

feed.title: ATI INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: ATI Inc. has released its annual 10-K report, revealing robust financial performance driven by strong demand in the aerospace and defense markets. Key financial metrics show increased sales, gross profit, operating income, and net income, with diluted net income per share reaching $2.85. The company's strategic initiatives include significant capital investments in aerospace and defense, capital management through share repurchases and debt reduction, and a focus on expanding production capacity for advanced metallic powders.

feed.title: Valaris Ltd SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Valaris Ltd released its 2025 10-K report, highlighting significant financial growth with net income up 165% and diluted EPS at $13.86, driven by higher operating income and tax benefits. The report also details a pending business combination with Transocean Ltd. and outlines strategic focuses on high-return assets and a joint venture with Saudi Aramco. Challenges include risks associated with the Transocean merger, operational dependencies on volatile commodity prices, cybersecurity threats, and regulatory complexities.

feed.title: Hormel sells whole-turkey business to another Minnesota company, but Jennie-O remains
feed.summary: Hormel Foods is selling its whole-bird turkey business to Willmar-based Life-Science Innovations, a move aimed at reducing exposure to volatile, commodity-driven businesses. While Hormel will retain the Jennie-O brand and continue to sell ground turkey, the sale includes a production facility and feed mill. This strategic decision comes as the turkey industry faces declining consumer demand and fluctuating commodity prices.

feed.title: S&P Global (SPGI) Enters a Strategic Data Sharing Agreement with Verisk
feed.summary: S&P Global Inc. (SPGI) has entered a strategic data-sharing agreement with Verisk to provide climate catastrophe risk analytics for the insurance and finance sectors. This partnership combines S&P's climate projections with Verisk's insurance modeling to help companies quantify potential losses from future climate events. S&P Global recently reported its Q4 2025 earnings, showing a 9.02% year-over-year revenue increase to $3.92 billion, driven by strong divisional performance.

feed.title: Perfect Stock Portfolio: February 2026 Edition
feed.summary: This article details the February 2026 Perfect Stock Portfolio, emphasizing disciplined value investing in a market transitioning from narrative-driven speculation to fundamental asset strength. The strategy focuses on acquiring strong balance sheets at discounts to tangible book value, as demonstrated by the exit from Kyocera (KYOCY) after a 123% gain. The portfolio currently includes global asset-heavy businesses in sectors like shipping, real estate, industrials, energy, and financial services, which are expected to benefit as markets increasingly prioritize tangible assets and predictable cash flow.

feed.title: SAP SE (SAP) to Raise Annual Dividend
feed.summary: SAP SE (SAP) has proposed a €0.15 per share increase in its annual dividend for fiscal year 2025, bringing it to €2.50 per share, a 6.4% year-over-year rise. This decision, if approved by shareholders, will result in a total payout of €2.919 billion, reflecting a payout ratio of 40.7%. The company recently reported strong fiscal Q4 2025 results, with significant growth in Cloud and Cloud ERP Suite revenues.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories (ABT) Announces Positive Results for Amulet 360 Left Atrial Appendage (LAA) Occlude
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories announced positive clinical trial results for its Amulet 360 Left Atrial Appendage (LAA) occluder. The VERITAS Study showed top-tier performance for patients with paroxysmal and persistent AFib, with 93.9% achieving complete LAA closure after 45 days and a 99.8% implant success rate. Full results for this device are expected in 2027.

feed.title: EQT buys larger stake in Mountain Valley Pipeline
feed.summary: EQT Corp., in partnership with Blackstone Credit & Insurance, will increase its ownership in the Mountain Valley Pipeline by exercising an option to acquire part of Con Edison's stake for approximately $115 million. This deal follows EQT's regaining partial ownership after acquiring Equitrans Midstream Corp. in 2024. The agreement was finalized on January 2nd and is expected to close in the first half of the year.

feed.title: $41,608,200 Raised on Initial Closing. Books Now Re-Open for a Second Closing on April 1, 2026
feed.summary: Maple Leaf Critical Minerals 2026 Enhanced Flow-Through Limited Partnership announced it has completed its initial closing, raising $41,608,200. The books are now reopened for a second closing on April 1, 2026. The partnership offers tax benefits through investments in critical minerals exploration across Canada, with specific advantages for National Class and Québec Class Unit holders.

feed.title: Hapag-Lloyd set to grow its intra-Asia network through Zim's Gold Star Line
feed.summary: Hapag-Lloyd is expanding its intra-Asia network through a collaboration with Zim's Gold Star Line. Gold Star Line operates a China-centric network with 20 liner services and 40 ships, serving Asia, India, and South Africa with a total capacity of 100,000 TEUs. This move will allow Hapag-Lloyd to leverage Gold Star Line's extensive presence in the region.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips exploring sale of $2B in Permian Basin assets, Bloomberg says
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) is reportedly exploring the sale of some Permian Basin assets, which are expected to generate approximately $2 billion. This move is part of a broader portfolio streamlining effort by the company. Following the news, shares of ConocoPhillips were trading 0.8% lower in afternoon trading.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Celebrates Ribbon Cutting at LaGrange East Location
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams held an official ribbon-cutting ceremony for its new LaGrange East location, marking the culmination of a project first announced in March 2025. The new store expanded access to their products for homeowners, contractors, and local businesses, and it officially opened its doors in October 2025. This celebration acknowledges Sherwin-Williams' continued investment in the LaGrange community.

feed.title: VivoPower secures $30M for AI data centers - ICYMI
feed.summary: VivoPower International PLC has secured $30 million in PIPE financing from strategic investors, including Blue Sky Capital, to pivot entirely towards AI data center development. The company is exiting its digital treasury and electric vehicle businesses to focus on land acquisition, permitting, construction, and leasing of AI data centers to hyperscalers. VivoPower sees massive global demand for AI infrastructure, particularly in the Nordics, GCC, and Korea, which they consider the "oil fields of the future."

feed.title: Four Seasons Resort Orlando at Walt Disney World sold as part of $1B deal
feed.summary: The Four Seasons Resort Orlando at Walt Disney World, a luxury hotel featuring a Michelin-starred restaurant, has been sold as part of a $1 billion deal. This transaction marks a significant event in the region's hotel market and indicates growing momentum in hotel transactions across the Americas. The resort is known for its premier amenities and views of Disney's fireworks.

feed.title: AMKOR TECHNOLOGY, INC. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Amkor Technology, Inc. has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing strong financial performance with net sales of $6,707.981 million and stable net income of $373.895 million. The report highlights strategic initiatives, including expanding manufacturing in Arizona and Vietnam, and significant investments in advanced packaging and test technologies. Despite market, operational, and regulatory risks, Amkor plans substantial capital expenditures for 2026 and aims for continued growth in key semiconductor markets.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Price Target for Devon Energy (DVN) to $51
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has reaffirmed its 'Overweight' rating for Devon Energy (DVN) and increased its price target from $43.00 to $51.00, an 18.60% rise. This upgrade follows several other positive analyst updates for DVN in recent months. The stock's average target price from 27 analysts is $48.45, implying a 9.40% upside from its current price, with a consensus "Outperform" recommendation.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Recruiting Loan Tab Nears $5 Billion
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's outstanding balance of forgivable recruiting loans for advisors increased by 12%, or $520 million, last year, reaching nearly $4.9 billion. This surge, detailed in the firm's annual SEC filing, indicates a continued aggressive recruitment strategy, contrasting with the prior year's flat growth in loan balances. The loans are forgiven over time as advisors remain with the firm, and the increase reflects a rise in new recruits, including large teams from competitors like UBS and Merrill Lynch.

feed.title: Key facts: RTX delays GTF engine shipments to Airbus; Raytheon gains Navy approval for StormBreaker
feed.summary: RTX is experiencing delays in shipping GTF engines to Airbus, leading Airbus to adopt a cautious production outlook for 2026 due to the lack of an agreement on delivery numbers. In other news, Raytheon, a subsidiary of RTX, has received U.S. Navy approval for its StormBreaker smart weapon to be used on the F/A-18E/F Super Hornet, enhancing its target engagement capabilities in various weather conditions.

feed.title: Key facts: Martin Marietta reports 9% revenue rise; announces dividend
feed.summary: Martin Marietta Materials Inc. announced a 9% rise in revenue to $6.15 billion in 2025, driven by increased shipments and strong aggregates pricing, resulting in a gross profit of $1.89 billion. The company also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.83 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 2, 2026.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Stock: Earnings Beat, Strong Guidance—Is the Rally Just Starting?
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) reported stronger-than-expected earnings and reaffirmed its full-year outlook, driven by resilient U.S. travel demand and premium cabin strength. Analysts are split on future upside, with some seeing continued growth due to strong fundamentals and debt reduction, while others caution about sector-specific risks like fuel prices and economic slowdowns. The article provides a detailed analysis of Delta's financial performance, market positioning, and analyst consensus, noting its role as a "quality pick" within the airline industry for investors seeking exposure to U.S. consumer spending.

feed.title: Generac Holdings Stock Outlook Improves
feed.summary: Generac Holdings' stock, after significant volatility between 2020 and 2021, is now showing signs of recovery. Analysts predict a continued upward trend, potentially reaching $325, driven by improving market sentiment and strong demand for renewable energy and portable generators. Investors are advised to add to their positions to capitalize on this anticipated growth.

feed.title: FDA accepts Teva NDA for olanzapine extended-release injectable suspension
feed.summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has accepted Teva Pharmaceuticals' New Drug Application (NDA) for an olanzapine extended-release injectable suspension (TEV-'749) for the treatment of schizophrenia in adults. This new formulation aims to improve treatment adherence and offer a long-acting option without the FDA-required Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) that other such formulations currently have. Phase 3 trials showed the once-monthly subcutaneous injection to have an efficacy and safety profile consistent with existing olanzapine formulations, without needing post-injection monitoring.

feed.title: Nucor promotes Jack Sullivan to CFO
feed.summary: Nucor Corp. has promoted John L. “Jack” Sullivan to the role of chief financial officer, treasurer, and executive VP, effective March 1. Sullivan, who joined Nucor in 2022, previously served as VP, treasurer, and general manager of investor relations. He succeeds Steve Laxton, who became president and COO on January 1.

feed.title: Mueller Industries Announces Sixth Consecutive Dividend Increase
feed.summary: Mueller Industries has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.35 per share, marking its sixth consecutive increase and a 40% rise over 2025. The dividend is scheduled for payment on March 27, 2026, with a record date of March 13, 2026. This reflects the company's sustained profitability and commitment to shareholder returns, as it continues to operate across major global markets in critical distribution sectors.

feed.title: Red Rock Resorts, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Red Rock Resorts, Inc. has released its 2025 10-K report, showcasing robust financial performance with net revenues of $2,011.5 million and net income up 22.1% to $355.7 million. Key highlights include the opening of Durango Casino & Resort and strategic expansion plans, alongside challenges such as economic uncertainties and regulatory risks. The company plans to focus on operational discipline and capital management, allocating $375 million to $425 million for capital expenditures in 2026.

feed.title: The role of AI and humanics in training healthcare professionals
feed.summary: This article discusses the integration of AI and humanics in healthcare education to prepare future professionals. It emphasizes the need for technological, data, and human literacy, asserting that human connection and empathy are crucial alongside technological advancements. Fairleigh Dickinson University's approach, focusing on five pillars of humanics, aims to create a healthcare workforce that balances AI's power with strong human grounding.

feed.title: LiveRamp to Integrate Scowtt's Predictive AI Models Into its Data Collaboration Platform
feed.summary: LiveRamp is integrating Scowtt's predictive AI models into its data collaboration platform to enhance real-time decision-making for customers using first-party data. This partnership aims to unlock AI-powered optimization by leveraging LiveRamp's privacy-centric data capabilities with Scowtt's AI, enabling brands to improve marketing efforts, customer engagement, and business outcomes. The collaboration will help businesses activate their data for more efficient and intelligent operations.

feed.title: Praxis Precision Shares Rally On Price Target Hikes: Analyst Sees Upside As High As 100% For Stock
feed.summary: Praxis Precision Medicines (PRAX) shares rallied after analysts increased their price targets following the company's FDA submissions for two experimental drugs, ulixacaltamide and relutrigine, both of which have Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Truist raised its price target to $700, citing the completed NDA submissions as "notable," while Wells Fargo expressed surprise that priority review wasn't requested for Ulixadolid despite its breakthrough status. Wedbush also increased its price target but maintained an 'Underperform' rating, holding reservations on the ulixacaltamide filing.

feed.title: Waystar Stock: Quiet Pullback, Big Questions After Healthcare IT Rally
feed.summary: Waystar (NASDAQ: WAY) shares have experienced a quiet pullback after an initial post-IPO rally, raising questions among investors despite strong underlying fundamentals in US healthcare IT. The company, which provides healthcare payments and revenue-cycle software, is positioned in a growing market where hospitals and physician groups need automation to optimize payments amid rising denials and labor costs. The article explores the reasons for the stock's recent softness, including software rotation, profit-taking, and investor reactions to broader healthcare commentary, while noting Wall Street analysts generally maintain a positive long-term outlook.

feed.title: A10 Networks (ATEN) Sees Analyst Price Target Raised by BWS Fina
feed.summary: BWS Financial has raised its price target for A10 Networks (ATEN) to $28.00, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows other recent analyst activity, including BTIG reiterating a Buy rating with a $22.00 target and Deutsche Bank initiating coverage with a Buy rating and $22.00 target. The average one-year price target from six analysts is currently $23.50, implying an upside of 17.85% from the current price.

feed.title: Arvinas, Inc. (ARVN) Stock Analysis: Unlocking Potential in Biotechnology with an 8.52% Upside
feed.summary: Arvinas, Inc. (ARVN) is a biotechnology company focused on developing therapies using the body’s natural protein disposal system, with its stock showing potential an 8.52% upside according to analysts. Despite facing financial challenges common for clinical-stage firms, Arvinas has a robust product pipeline including therapies for prostate and breast cancer in Phase 3 trials and strong partnerships with major pharmaceutical companies. Technical indicators suggest the stock is oversold, while analyst sentiment remains cautiously optimistic, making it a compelling opportunity for risk-tolerant investors.

feed.title: BUILDING MATERIALS DRIVERS AT HOME DEPOT-OWNED TEMCO LOGISTICS JOIN TEAMSTERS
feed.summary: Nearly 100 drivers at Temco Logistics, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Home Depot, have voted to join Teamsters Local 528, marking the first union organizing victory at any Home Depot company. This victory comes despite an intense anti-union campaign from the company, with drivers seeking better pay, dignity, and respect at work. The Teamsters General President Sean M. O'Brien stated that these workers are taking a history-defining step and will prove the union's power when they win their first contract.
